issuesFound.title: Missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is present but empty. Screen reader users rely on the page title to understand the page's purpose and distinguish it from other pages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools for every team</title>

issuesFound.title: Skip link target missing
issuesFound.explanation: A skip link is intended to help keyboard users bypass repetitive navigation, but the target element (typically main content) is missing an id that matches the fragment identifier in the skip link's href. This makes the skip link non-functional.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires adding id="main-content" to the main element and updating the skip link href)

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is not descriptive out of context. Screen reader users often navigate by links, and generic phrases like "read more" do not convey the link's destination or purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 177: Read more (no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element is missing an alt attribute. All images must have an alt attribute to convey their purpose or content to screen reader users. Without it, the image is completely invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 201:

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The <input> element lacks an accessible label. Screen reader users need labels to understand the purpose of form controls. Without a label, the input is announced only by its type ("edit" or "textbox"), making it difficult to use.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: <input type="email" id="contact-email" name="email" autocomplete="work email" required aria-required="true" aria-label="Work email address">

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple <nav> elements exist on the page, but this one lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute to distinguish it from others. When navigating by landmarks, screen reader users cannot tell which navigation region they are entering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 658: <nav aria-label="Footer navigation">

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button controls a dropdown menu but lacks aria-expanded to indicate its state (expanded/collapsed). Screen reader users need this attribute to understand whether the menu is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 39: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu" aria-label="Toggle main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element does not specify a scope attribute. Without scope, screen reader users navigating the table may not understand which row or column the header applies to, especially in complex tables.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Page title empty
issuesFound.explanation: The title element is empty. Browsers and assistive technologies rely on the title to identify the page. An empty title makes orientation and navigation difficult for all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The section element uses aria-labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists in the document. Assistive technologies cannot compute the accessible name for this landmark.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 160:
Line 162:

Everything you need to ship accessible software

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button controls the visibility of the navigation menu but lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen-reader users cannot tell whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 37: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu">

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button contains only an aria-hidden icon and has no accessible name. Screen-reader users will hear only "button" with no indication of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 37: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Open navigation menu" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu">

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy jumps from h2 directly to h5 in the "How it works" section, skipping h3 and h4. This disrupts the logical outline and makes navigation confusing for screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 186:

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The table header cell lacks a scope attribute. Without scope, screen readers cannot reliably associate this header with its corresponding data cells.
issuesFound.fix: Line 461: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The img element is missing an alt attribute entirely. Because the image is not marked as decorative, assistive technologies have no text alternative to announce.

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The visible link text is "Read more", which does not communicate the destination or purpose of the link. Users navigating by links list or speech recognition cannot distinguish this link from other generic links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: Start your free trial(no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has no associated label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen-reader users will not know the purpose of this field when they encounter it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 702: <input type="email" id="newsletter-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" placeholder="you@example.com" aria-label="Email address">

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The first-name input collects personal information but lacks an autocomplete attribute. Browsers and assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the input purpose, making form filling harder for users with cognitive or motor disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 747: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple nav elements, and this instance has neither an aria-label nor an aria-labelledby attribute. Assistive-technology users navigating by landmarks cannot tell this navigation region apart from the others.
